name: Wireless earbuds
summary: True wireless in-ear headphones, $40-$350, with and without ANC.

evidence.claim: Earbud battery claims typically state 'total with case' — the earbuds themselves typically run 4-8 hours; the case provides 3-5 full recharges.
IPX4 rating is sweat-resistant (splash-resistant); IPX7 is waterproof to 1m for 30 min; IP68 is full immersion. Marketing copy often conflates.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Total-with-case figure. Earbuds themselves run 5-8 hours; rest comes from 3-4 case recharges (E1). For 'won't die during my commute' durability, the earbud-only figure is what matters.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the battery figure specify earbud-only vs total-with-case?

confabulationPatterns.reality: IPX4 = sweat-resistant only; splash from water bottle OK but immersion not. True waterproof is IPX7+ (E2). For pools or shower, need IPX7+.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What specific IP rating? Anything <IPX7 is not waterproof.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Not measurable. Codec support, EQ customization, and driver type matter more. LDAC on Android + AirPods-level ANC is the current frontier.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is 'best-in-class' substantiated with frequency response or codec support?

counterfeitSignals.signal: AirPods Pro listed >30% below Apple price from marketplace seller
counterfeitSignals.action: flag — counterfeit AirPods are widespread; serial number verification on Apple's site is required

compatibilityQuestions: iOS or Android? AAC codec is iOS-optimal; LDAC/aptX is Android-optimal.
Will you work out / shower / swim with them? Determines IP rating requirement.
Do you wear glasses? Some earbud shapes fit poorly under glasses arms.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Replacement ear tips (sizes for better fit)

repairability.notes: Almost no earbuds are serviceable. Battery-replacement programs exist for AirPods at ~$49 but most brands are replace-don't-repair.
